edits_required.action: Update status and add CTO ruling
edits_required.detail: OQ-3 is a required gate before M1 release build, not an open-ended M1 question. Update Status from 'OPEN' to 'OPEN — required gate before M1 release build'. Add CTO ruling text: 'CTO call: this is not acceptable to carry open through M1 implementation. The actual Ed25519 public key PEM (OpenSSH/PKCS8 format, ~119 chars base64 body + headers + newlines) is materially longer than the 97-char Case B tested in SPIKE-002. Staff-Engineer must run a Test 6 variant with a full-length real PEM string (>500 chars including headers and escaped newlines) before M1 release build is cut. This is a day-1 M1 sprint gate, not a day-1 measurement — it blocks the release build step, not implementation start.'

The current neutral consequence ('Bun must be installed directly on dev machines via https://bun.sh/install or Homebrew') is sufficient as a statement. But link it forward to the M1 build setup doc: append 'Exact steps documented in the M1 build setup doc (Future Work — M1 day-1 deliverable).'

items_confirmed_accurate: Pinned versions match SPIKE-002 verdict (bun 1.3.14, ink 7.0.4, react 19.2.0, typescript 5.4.5, ink-text-input 6.0.0, ink-spinner 5.0.0)
Binary size claims (~65MB macOS / ~96MB Linux) match SPIKE-002 Test 4 and Test 5 evidence
SOC2 chain rationale (compiled-in key via bun build --define) is consistent with ADR-0001 D2 and ADR-0004
Stack A Python _keys.py ruling is technically correct — file-based constant defeats ADR-0001 D2 compiled-in key requirement
Ink-on-Bun not officially supported claim remains accurate — SPIKE-002 GO does not change Ink maintainer posture
ADR-0001 OQ-4 resolution via SPIKE-002 Test 6 is correctly characterized
